title: Aiko's First Day Friends
synopsis: When shy Aiko starts her first day at preschool, she feels scared and hides from the other children. But when she discovers the class pet bunny is also frightened, she finds the courage to help and makes her first friend in the process.

pages.text: Aiko holds Mama's hand tight. Today is her first day at preschool. She feels scared. The big building has bright yellow doors and colorful flowers.

illustration.description: A small Japanese girl with shoulder-length black hair in a red backpack holds her mother's hand in front of a cheerful preschool building. Aiko looks nervous with wide eyes, while her mother smiles encouragingly. The building has bright yellow doors and rainbow flower boxes.
illustration.alt: Aiko holding her mother's hand in front of preschool

pages.text: Inside, children are playing everywhere. They look happy and loud. Aiko hides behind Mama. Her tummy feels funny. She wants to go home.

illustration.description: A busy preschool classroom with diverse children playing with blocks, painting, and reading. Aiko peeks out from behind her mother's legs, looking worried. The room is bright with rainbow decorations and cheerful artwork on the walls.
illustration.alt: Aiko hiding behind her mother in a busy classroom

pages.text: Mama says goodbye with a hug. Now Aiko is alone. She sits in the quiet corner. Miss Chen, her teacher, has kind eyes and curly hair.

illustration.description: Aiko sits alone in a reading corner with soft pillows and books, looking sad. Miss Chen, a friendly Asian teacher with curly black hair and a warm smile, approaches gently with her hand extended. Soft morning light streams through windows.
illustration.alt: Aiko sitting alone while Miss Chen approaches kindly

pages.text: In the corner, Aiko sees a white bunny. The bunny is hiding too! His name is Snowy. He looks scared, just like Aiko feels.

illustration.description: Close-up of Aiko kneeling next to a large cage with a fluffy white bunny huddled in the corner. Both Aiko and Snowy have similar worried expressions. The cage has soft bedding and a small food bowl.
illustration.alt: Aiko discovering the scared bunny Snowy

pages.text: Aiko whispers softly to Snowy. She knows how he feels. Being new is scary. But maybe they can be scared together.

illustration.description: Aiko sits cross-legged in front of the bunny cage, speaking softly to Snowy. Her expression shows understanding and gentleness. Snowy's ears perk up slightly, showing he's listening. The background is soft and warm.
illustration.alt: Aiko talking gently to Snowy the bunny

pages.text: Aiko opens the cage door slowly. She feels brave now. Snowy needs her help. She pets him gently. His fur is soft and warm.

illustration.description: Aiko carefully reaches into the bunny cage, her face showing concentration and courage. Snowy allows her to pet him, looking less frightened. The scene shows trust building between them with warm, golden lighting.
illustration.alt: Aiko bravely petting Snowy in his cage

pages.text: A boy named Marcus sees Aiko with Snowy. 'Can I pet him too?' he asks nicely. Marcus has dark skin and a big smile. Soon, other children come over.

illustration.description: Marcus, a cheerful Black boy with a bright smile, approaches Aiko and Snowy. Behind them, other diverse children gather curiously - a girl with braids, a boy with freckles, and others. Everyone looks friendly and excited.
illustration.alt: Marcus and other children joining Aiko and Snowy

pages.text: Now Aiko feels happy! She helps the other children pet Snowy safely. They all laugh when Snowy wiggles his nose. Aiko made friends by being brave and kind.

illustration.description: A group of diverse preschoolers sitting in a circle around Aiko and Snowy. Everyone is smiling and laughing. Aiko looks confident and happy in the center. Snowy appears relaxed and content. The classroom is filled with joy and friendship.
illustration.alt: Aiko happily surrounded by new friends and Snowy

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Aiko feel at the beginning of school?
questions.explanation: Aiko felt scared on her first day of preschool and hid behind her mama.

questions.question: What was the bunny's name?
questions.explanation: The white bunny's name was Snowy.

questions.question: Who was the first child to ask about petting Snowy?
questions.explanation: Marcus was the boy who asked nicely if he could pet Snowy too.

questions.question: How did Aiko feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Aiko felt happy because she made friends by being brave and helping Snowy.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Page 1
pages.text: Aiko holds Mama's hand tight. She feels _____. The bunny is _____ too!

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Page 2
pages.text: Aiko feels _____ when she helps Snowy. She makes new _____ at school.
